title: HTML Fundamentals
shortDescription: Structure your first web pages from scratch — elements, semantics, links, images and forms.

lessons.title: What is HTML?
lessons.content:
# What is HTML? HTML (HyperText Markup Language) is the standard language used to create the structure of every web page. It tells the browser what each piece of content **is** — a heading, a paragraph, a link, an image — not how it should look. ## Elements and tags An HTML element is written using a pair of tags: ```html

This is a paragraph.

``` The opening tag `

` marks the start, and the closing tag `

` marks the end. Some elements, like ``, don't need a closing tag. :::tip Browsers ignore extra whitespace and line breaks in your HTML, so indent your code for readability — it won't change how the page looks. ::: ## Why HTML matters Every website you have ever visited — no matter the framework or language behind it — sends HTML to your browser in the end. Learning HTML well makes everything else in web development easier.

lessons.title: Your First HTML Page
lessons.content:
# Your First HTML Page Every HTML document follows the same basic skeleton. :::example ```html My First Page

Hello, world!

This is my very first web page.

``` ::: - `` tells the browser this is an HTML5 document. - `` wraps the entire page. - `` holds metadata (like the page title) that isn't shown directly on the page. - `` holds everything visible to the visitor. :::try Edit the HTML below and press Run to see it rendered live. ```html

Hello, world!

Change this text and hit run.

``` :::

lessons.title: Headings and Paragraphs
lessons.content:
# Headings and Paragraphs HTML gives you six levels of headings, `

` through `

`, in decreasing order of importance, plus the `

` tag for paragraphs. ```html

Main Page Title

A Section Heading

A paragraph of body text goes here.

``` :::tip Use only one `

` per page — it should describe what the whole page is about. Use lower-level headings to organize sections underneath it. :::

lessons.title: Lists and Links
lessons.content:
# Lists and Links ## Lists Use `
    ` for an unordered (bulleted) list and `
      ` for an ordered (numbered) one. Each item goes inside `
    1. `. ```html
      • HTML
      • CSS
      • JavaScript
      ``` ## Links The anchor tag `` creates a hyperlink using the `href` attribute. ```html Visit Example ``` :::try ```html
      • Learn HTML
      • Learn CSS
      Visit Dexterity Learn ``` :::

lessons.title: Images
lessons.content: # Images The `` tag embeds an image. It's a self-closing tag and always needs a `src` (source) and `alt` (alternative text for accessibility and SEO). ```html ``` :::tip Never skip the `alt` attribute — screen readers rely on it, and so does Google Image Search. :::

lessons.title: Try It Yourself: Build a Bio Page
lessons.content:
# Try It Yourself: Build a Bio Page Put together everything from this module — headings, a paragraph, a list and a link — into one small bio page. :::try ```html

Hi, I'm Alex

I'm learning web development with Dexterity Learn.

  • Currently learning: HTML
  • Next up: CSS
See my progress ``` ::: Once you're happy with your page, mark this lesson complete below to finish the course.

quiz.question: Which attribute provides alternative text for an image?
How many heading levels does HTML provide?
Which tag holds everything visible on the page?

title: CSS Foundations
shortDescription: Style pages with confidence — selectors, the box model, flexbox and responsive basics.

lessons.title: What is CSS?
Selectors
The Box Model
Flexbox Basics
lessons.content:
# Flexbox Basics Flexbox makes it easy to align and distribute items along a row or column. ```css .row { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; } ``` :::try ```html
Box 1
Box 2
Box 3
``` :::

quiz.question: Which is the correct order of the box model, inside out?
Which property turns an element into a flex container?
Which selector targets elements with a given class?

title: JavaScript Essentials
shortDescription: Bring your pages to life — variables, control flow, functions and hands-on practice.

lessons.title: Variables and Data Types
Operators
lessons.content:
# Operators

```js
let sum = 4 + 5;        // arithmetic
let isEqual = (4 === 4); // strict equality
let isBig = (10 > 5);    // comparison
```

:::tip
Always use `===` (strict equality) instead of `==` to avoid unexpected type coercion bugs.
:::

:::try
```js
console.log(2 + 3 * 4);
console.log(10 % 3);
console.log(5 === "5");
console.log(5 === 5);
```
:::

lessons.title: Conditionals
Loops
Writing Functions
Try It Yourself: FizzBuzz

quiz.question: Which keyword declares a variable that cannot be reassigned?
What does === check that == does not?
options: Value type as well as value

title: Python for Beginners
shortDescription: A gentle, practical introduction to Python syntax, variables and control flow.

lessons.title: Introduction to Python
lessons.content:
# Introduction to Python

Python is a general-purpose language known for its clean, readable syntax — a great first language.

```python
print("Hello, world!")
```

Unlike some languages, Python uses **indentation** (spacing) instead of curly braces to mark blocks of code — so consistent indentation isn't just style, it's required.

:::tip
Use 4 spaces per indentation level. Most editors do this automatically when you press Tab in a Python file.
:::

lessons.title: Variables & Types
lessons.content:
# Variables & Types

```python
name = "Priya"
age = 21
is_student = True
```

Python's core types include `str`, `int`, `float`, and `bool`. You can check a variable's type with the built-in `type()` function.

```python
print(type(age))  # <class 'int'>
```

:::example
```python
name = "Priya"
age = 21
print(f"{name} is {age} years old")
```
:::

lessons.title: If Statements
lessons.content:
# If Statements

```python
score = 82

if score >= 90:
    print("Grade: A")
elif score >= 75:
    print("Grade: B")
else:
    print("Grade: C")
```

:::tip
Notice there are no parentheses around the condition and no curly braces — just a colon and indentation.
:::

lessons.title: Loops
lessons.content:
# Loops

```python
for i in range(1, 6):
    print("Count:", i)
```

```python
n = 3
while n > 0:
    print(n)
    n -= 1
```

:::tip
`range(1, 6)` produces 1 through 5 — the end value is never included.
:::

quiz.question: What replaces curly braces in Python to define code blocks?
Which function prints a variable's type?
Which keyword starts a Python conditional branch after the first?
